page: Microsoft Azure E16pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine belonging to the Epdsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated physical cores and 16 vCPUs powered by a Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor operating at 3.4 GHz. The CPU architecture is arm64, configured with a 128 MB L3 cache, 1.0 MB L2 cache, and 64 KB L1d cache. The server is equipped with 128.0 GB of memory, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It includes 945 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, with no bundled GPUs or public IPv4 addresses.

In benchmark evaluations, the E16pds_v6 demonstrates strong memory performance, achieving a top-tier rating in the PassMark memory score. Large-block uncached memory bandwidth is measured at 304.72 GB/sec for read operations and 123.05 GB/sec for write operations, both placing in the top 25% tier. Computational performance is solid, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 33,147.69 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 14,685.0, both aligning with average tier expectations. Single-threaded gzip compression performs strongly at 33.05 GB/sec, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression reaches 175.98 GB/sec.

The server exhibits a clear performance tradeoff in large language model inference tasks. While prompt processing for a small 135M model reaches an average speed of 606.2 tokens/sec, processing prompts for a medium 7B model drops to a weak tier of 24.6 tokens/sec. Given its high memory density of 8.0 GB per core, top-tier memory bandwidth, and 945 GB of local storage, the E16pds_v6 is structured for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, and caching systems such as Redis, where it achieves an average of 31.61 million SET operations per second.
description: Microsoft Azure E16pds_v6 is an ARM-based virtual machine from the Epdsv6 family, featuring 16 dedicated physical cores on a 3.4 GHz Microsoft Neoverse-N2 processor. Configured with 128.0 GB of system memory (8.0 GB per core) and 945 GB of local storage, this instance operates on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It delivers strong memory bandwidth, with uncached large-block reads at 304.72 GB/sec and writes at 123.05 GB/sec, earning a top-tier PassMark memory rating. While multi-core CPU benchmarks like stress-ng and Geekbench sit in the average tier, the server excels in memory-intensive tasks and data decompression, with multi-threaded bzip3 reaching 175.98 GB/sec. However, LLM inference on larger models presents a performance tradeoff, with 7B model prompt processing falling into the weak tier. This server is optimized for memory-heavy workloads, database operations, and caching applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E16pds_v6 is an ARM64 virtual machine featuring 16 Neoverse-N2 cores, 128 GB RAM, and 945 GB local storage, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth for database and memory-intensive workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E16pds_v6 is an ARM64 VM with 16 Neoverse-N2 cores, 128 GB RAM, and 945 GB local storage, optimized for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 virtual machine featuring high memory density, top-tier memory bandwidth, and local storage for data-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: 16 dedicated Microsoft Neoverse-N2 ARM64 cores running at 3.4 GHz
128 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB per core
945 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Top-tier memory performance with 304.72 GB/sec large-block read bandwidth
Best suited for database operations, caching systems, and memory-intensive applications
